mission.title: Install and Configure Score on Kubernetes
mission.description: The Score Specification provides a developer-centric and platform-agnostic workload specification to improve developer productivity and experience. Installing Score allows you to define and manage your workloads in a consistent manner across different environments.

steps.title: Add the Score Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Score Helm repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add score https://charts.score.dev
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Score using Helm
steps.description:
Install the Score chart into your Kubernetes cluster. This command will create a namespace called 'score' if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install score score/score --version 0.3.0 --namespace score --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Score pods are running in the 'score' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace score
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
You may want to configure resource limits for the Score deployment. Edit the deployment as follows:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment score --namespace score
```
Add resource limits under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
```

steps.title: Access the Score service
steps.description:
To access the Score service, you can port-forward the service to your local machine:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/score --namespace score 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Score pods running in the 'score' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via port-forwarding. You can also verify that the resource limits have been applied correctly.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add score https://charts.score.dev
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
